headline: UAE MGX closes $49B AI fund
detail: One of the largest AI-focused investment vehicles ever signals sustained institutional conviction in AI infrastructure buildout. This directly benefits your chip holdings - SMH, SOXL, and names like NVDA that power AI compute. Keeps the macro bid under your entire book.
rec: No specific trade triggered - you already own SMH and SOXL which are the right vehicles. Just confirms holding is correct; no need to add more leveraged exposure on top.

headline: Anthropic gets export clearance ahead of IPO
detail: Trump administration lifting export controls on Anthropic's latest models removes a regulatory overhang for US AI companies broadly. It signals a more permissive AI export environment which is a tailwind for chip makers who supply foreign AI buildouts. Good for CRWD indirectly as security demand follows AI expansion.
rec: No action needed - this is a confirming tailwind for CRWD and SMH you already hold, not a new entry signal. Monitor CRWD above your $590 stop; it's up 6% and holding well.

headline: Microsoft planning thousands of layoffs while spending on AI
detail: MSFT cutting headcount while ramping AI capex means the AI infrastructure trade stays intact but software/services employment is under pressure. For your book the net read is neutral-to-positive: chip demand from hyperscaler AI spending continues, which supports SMH and SOXL.
rec: No trade warranted directly from this headline. Your SMH exposure already captures the beneficiary side of this trade. No scanner setup aligns to a new entry here.

headline: Tech leads first-half gains but biggest winners not US
detail: International tech outperforming is a mild relative warning - if rotation into non-US tech accelerates, US mega-cap and chip names could see some profit-taking. Your book is 90% concentrated in exactly the names that led and could face rotation pressure if this trend continues into Q3.
rec: No immediate trade, but worth watching SMH and QQQ for any breakdown below near-term support. Your stops on QQQ at $670 and SMH at $540 are your safety net - trust them if rotation hits.
